title: Kibbud Av Va'Em: Honoring Parents in Jewish Law
title_zh: 孝敬父母:犹太律法中的Kibbud Av Va'Em
title_he: כיבוד אב ואם: חובת כיבוד הורים בהלכה היהודית
chinese_parallels: 孝 (Filial piety) - Confucian virtue of honoring parents
儒家思想 - Confucian emphasis on family hierarchy and respect
Commercial similarities between Jewish Kibbud and Chinese Xiao in caring for aging parents
Both traditions recognize parents as source of existence and foundation of morality
key_concepts: Appears on first tablet of Ten Commandments (between man and God)
Three partners in creation: father, mother, and God
Limits: never violate Torah law for a parent
Protection from abuse while maintaining material obligations
Reward of 'long life' in both worlds
sources: Shulchan Aruch, Yoreh De'ah 240 - Legal code
Stories of Dama ben Netina, Rabbi Tarfon
Maimonides, Mishneh Torah, Laws of Rebels 6:1-13
target_audience: Chinese native Mandarin speakers, converts, new learners of Judaism
historical_period_references: Mount Sinai revelation (3,300 years ago / Late Shang Dynasty 商朝)
practical_applications: Providing food, clothing, and assistance to aging parents
Behavioral respect: not sitting in parent's seat, not contradicting publicly
When to refuse parental requests (Torah violations)
Post-death obligations: Kaddish, yahrzeit, visiting graves
